hero.headline.value: I ship systems where agents write the code — I build the tooling to trust them in production.

hero.intro.value: I'm Bobby. I became a software engineer by chasing the parts of code that feel like math — type systems, proofs, recursion, the self-referential knots where a thing contains itself and still holds together. I go down those paths because they're beautiful, not because anyone asked; the useful part shows up on its own. Lately it showed up as agents.

hero.summary.value: At Aura, I write production commerce code with AI assistance, backed by a contract-and-validation layer I built that enforces correctness in CI. This work is available in open-source form as prx and the bounded-systems libraries.

seeking.focus.value: Founding / senior engineering on agent infrastructure — agent safety and code provenance

seeking.detail.value: Senior engineering on teams building agentic products. I bring direct experience shipping agent-authored systems and the guardrails that make them trustworthy in production. Brooklyn · NYC · remote.

exp.bounded-systems.what.value: Capability security for AI agents — software delivered by agents, governed like infrastructure. The same discipline I ship at Aura, generalised into open tooling.

exp.bounded-systems.b0.value: Designed and built a capability-security toolchain — prx, guest-room, claude-box — that bounds what an AI agent can do by the scoped door references it holds: authority is the reference, not a string-matched rule, so an agent can only do what its references allow — nothing it can reach by guessing a name.

exp.bounded-systems.b1.value: Bound privileged actions to a signed owner and an auditable in-toto/SLSA provenance chain (ocap-provenance) — who authorized what is enforced in CI, not by convention.

exp.aura.what.value: On Commerce — the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems. I ship the integration and automation backbone, and built the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing.

exp.aura.b0.value: Ship the commerce and supply-chain backbone — NetSuite, Amazon, and Shopify integrations; inbound, Tactical, and LTL automation; EDI; and fulfillment daemons.

exp.aura.b1.value: Replaced Aura's spreadsheet-driven supply plan (the system behind inventory-purchasing) with one source of truth that compiles into every downstream artifact — schemas, docs, tests, agent context — with CI blocking anything that drifts out of sync.

exp.aura.b2.value: Built the auditing in: generated outputs are checked for provenance and against their contract, and sign-off is bound to an owner role enforced in CI rather than to a person.

exp.aura.b3.value: The changes themselves are written by AI agents, moving through that same pipeline to a reviewed PR — their output held to the same provenance and contract checks as everything else.

exp.l2l.what.value: Built the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team, spanning static-analysis CI, a design system, and frontend performance.

exp.l2l.b0.value: Built static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline to block defect classes before merge; over the same period, critical bugs dropped 20% and high-priority issues 25% across the 20-engineer team.

exp.l2l.b1.value: Shipped the frontend platform: a design system that cut task lead time 30%, a dashboard framework that took widget builds from a week to a day, and pagination and performance work that brought First Contentful Paint under a second.

exp.pioneer-works.what.value: Built the events + revenue data layer for the arts foundation.

exp.pioneer-works.b0.value: A unified CRM + Square-API ETL — cut payment-tracking time 80%, with P&L across 200+ events.

exp.kaleida-studio.what.value: Built just-in-time production automation for the fabrication studio.

exp.kaleida-studio.b0.value: Just-in-time production workflows — eliminated edition-numbering errors, from 10/month to zero.

exp.the-prepared.what.value: Built the publishing pipeline for the manufacturing publication.

exp.the-prepared.b0.value: A unified content pipeline — cut curation time 70% across 1000+ articles.

exp.recurse-center.what.value: Type theory in Haskell — using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. The intellectual root of the capability work: push correctness into the boundary so violations can't compile.

exp.recurse-center.b0.value: Type-driven design in Haskell — algebraic data types and typeclasses — the same instinct now applied to security seams: invalid states unrepresentable, sign-off un-forgeable.

work.prx.value: The agent-run work-unit CLI: capability-scoped agents whose privileged effects are verified against their signed owner, driving a work unit through one signed pipeline to a merged PR.

work.guest-room.value: Guest-agnostic room+door capability runtime — an agent reaches the outside world only through the door references it's handed.

work.claude-box.value: A capability-secured box for agent sessions — its authority is the door references it holds (keeper/scout/concierge/net), parent-agnostic.

work.door-kit.value: In-box door-client SDK for claude-box's capability doors (keeper/scout/concierge/spawn), over the guest-room protocol.

work.ocap-provenance.value: Capability-use provenance — a schema + SLSA mapping binding each privileged effect to a signed owner and an auditable chain.

work.string-audit.value: Cost-aware, grounded content auditor — typed string symbols, type-scoped audits, CAS-memoized LLM calls.
